frameworks.name: divisive normalization
frameworks.one_line: value coding circuit normalizes options against recent reward context

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: decision sensitivity adapts to reward distribution statistics

frameworks.name: multiple timescale dynamical model
frameworks.one_line: novel value representation across several adaptation timescales

frameworks.name: adaptive value coding
frameworks.one_line: neural value signals rescale to recent reward history

core_question: How does adaptive normalization of value representations across multiple timescales shape choice behavior under varying reward statistics?
key_assumptions: value coding adapts like sensory coding
reward history is integrated over multiple timescales
no explicit knowledge of environmental statistics needed
key_findings: nonhuman primate choice behavior varies with recent reward statistics
lower variance reward environments yield higher choice sensitivity
multiple timescale normalization model captures average adaptation
model also explains across-session behavioral variability
interpretations: value coding follows efficient coding principles via divisive normalization
dynamic architectures capture environmental statistics implicitly
